spotlights.title: Inception
spotlights.overview: Recorded on January 10–11, 1962, at Rudy Van Gelder’s studio, Inception marked McCoy Tyner’s debut as a leader for Impulse! Records, introducing a cohesive new trio with bassist Art Davis and drummer Elvin Jones, both colleagues from the Coltrane ensemble, whose familiarity lends the session its focused clarity. Across a six-track program featuring four Tyner originals (“Inception,” “Sunset,” “Effendi,” and “Blues for Gwen”) alongside “There Is No Greater Love” and “Speak Low,” the trio setting

spotlights.title: Birth Of The Cool
spotlights.overview: Miles Davis’ earliest masterpiece Birth Of The Cool gets the royal treatment in celebration of the legendary trumpeter’s Centennial. Following his trial-by-fire tenure in the Charlie Parker Quintet in the mid-1940s, Davis emerged with bolstered confidence and acreative hunger to explore new directions. A regular gathering of like-minded musicians at arranger Gil Evans’ Manhattan apartment provided the workshop at which the Miles Davis Nonet was born, and in 1949-50 Davis led three seminal sessio

spotlights.title: Everybody Else Is Doing It, So Why Can’t We?
spotlights.overview: Celebrating the landmark debut album from Ireland's favorite alternative band, The Cranberries. CD Deluxe: Featuring newly remastered audio from the album's original producer, Stephen Street, all done at Abbey Road Studios in London. 2CD Deluxe: This special 2CD Deluxe Edition features newly remastered audio from the album's original producer, Stephen Street, as well as a completely new mix of the album, all done at Abbey Road Studios in London. Remastered 2026 editions across CD and 2CD Deluxe

spotlights.title: 35 Biggest Hits
spotlights.overview: One of the most prolific self-directed creative forces in country's modern era. Keith amassed 42 top 10 hits, 33 No. 1s, more than 44 million albums sold. In 2008, 35 Biggest Hits was released on 2CDs, collecting 34 hits released between 1993 and 2006 and a new song.

spotlights.title: Gish
spotlights.overview: Spiritual debut and one of the best rock albums of the 1990s. Features the singles Siva, I Am One and Rhinoceros. Presented on 180-gram vinyl with original 1991 packaging for the 35th anniversary.

spotlights.title: Out of Exile
spotlights.overview: Newly pressed on 180-gram black vinyl. Newly remastered by Levi Seitz from Black Belt Mastering. Second studio album from Audioslave, led by Your Time Has Come, Out Of Exile and Be Yourself.

spotlights.title: In My Own Words
spotlights.overview: Ne-Yo is celebrating the 20th Anniversary of his debut In My Own Words. The 20th anniversary of Ne-Yo’s In My Own Words. This breakthrough album was critically acclaimed, debuting at No. 1 on the US Billboard 200, selling over 300,000 units, and earning a Grammy nomination for Best Contemporary R&B Album. The original album has been remixed and remastered as a 2LP and a CD reissue with bonus tracks. U.S. tour dates: July 10 - August 21

spotlights.title: Brother Where You Bound / Free As A Bird
spotlights.overview: Half-speed masters, 180g black vinyl. Supertramp are keeping their vinyl reissue series rolling with the announcement of two more releases: 1985’sBrother Where You Bound and 1987’s Free As A Bird. The audio for both albums was transferred at Abbey Road Studios by Miles Showell. He cut the half-speed master used to press the records, which can be heard on 180g black vinyl. Brother Where You Bound, the British rock band’s eighth album, marked their first LP following original member Roger Hodgson’

spotlights.title: Carry On
spotlights.overview: Cornell's second solo studio album released on vinyl for the first time. Newly remastered by Levi Seitz at Blackbelt Mastering and pressed on 180-gram black vinyl. Includes bonus tracks Today and Roads We Choose, plus an unreleased extended intro for Billie Jean and the Bond theme You Know My Name.
